Bring data and a brief.

Paste your numbers, name the insight you want to land, and Canopy renders an editorial-quality visualisation — never a default chart.

Recent in project

How Canopy works

From a paste of data to an export-ready chart, in five steps.

1
Paste your dataDrop in a CSV, an Excel file, or copy and paste raw numbers straight from a spreadsheet. Headers are optional and Canopy will figure out the structure.
2
Describe the pointWrite one sentence on what you want the chart to say. Not a description of the data but the insight. "The North is outperforming every other region" is more useful than "regional sales data." Canopy reads this to help pick the right chart — and if you want it to appear as the chart title, toggle "Show as title" on before generating.
3
Canopy picks the best chartBased on your data and what you've written, Canopy selects the most appropriate chart type and flags it as the Lead pick with a plain-English explanation of why.
4
Browse your optionsUse the arrows to move through five ranked options. The first two are close alternatives to the lead and the last two are more unexpected takes on the same data.
5
Export and use itOnce you've found the one you want, copy the HTML to embed it anywhere or download a standalone HTML or SVG ready to drop straight into a deck or document.
